data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: evictUnderperformingValidators() is invoked from Reconfiguration, but from _applyReconfigurationInternal paths at lines 99 and 151 (before DKG), NOT from _applyReconfiguration() at the old line 274. Comment at line 285 explicitly notes 'evictUnderperformingValidators() is now called BEFORE DKG starts, so its readings naturally precede this reset.'
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Call site is now pre-DKG; original's line reference (274) is outdated.

data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: getAllPerformances() returns an array whose length equals _validators.length (number of slots last seeded in onNewEpoch). ValidatorManagement reads it into memory perfs and computes perfLen = perfs.length at line 676.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: None at this step; pure view read.

data_flow_trace.original: If perfLen < activeLen, checkLen = perfLen (silent min() fallback)
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The min() fallback described in the issue DOES NOT EXIST in current code. Instead, a strict equality check is applied: `if (activeLen != perfLen) { emit PerformanceLengthMismatch(activeLen, perfLen); return; }`. Any length mismatch (either perfLen < activeLen or perfLen > activeLen) emits an explicit on-chain event and aborts Phase 2 entirely without marking anyone as evicted.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: activeLen == perfLen required to proceed to the eviction loop.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: The vulnerable `uint256 checkLen = activeLen < perfLen ? activeLen : perfLen;` pattern has been replaced with strict equality + event + early return.

data_flow_trace.original: Loop only iterates to checkLen; validators at [perfLen, activeLen) are never checked
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The loop iterates `for (uint256 i = 0; i < activeLen; i++)` using perfs[i] directly. This loop is only reached when activeLen == perfLen (guaranteed by the gate at 679). There is no checkLen variable; no partial iteration path exists.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Only reachable when activeLen == perfLen; otherwise function returns at line 681.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: No min() bound; loop bound is activeLen and is only entered when equality holds.

data_flow_trace.original: Trailing validators escape eviction silently, no event emitted
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: On mismatch, `PerformanceLengthMismatch(activeCount, perfCount)` is explicitly emitted (event declared at IValidatorManagement.sol:77) and Phase 2 aborts for ALL validators, not just trailing ones. There is no silent partial-coverage behavior; no validator escapes eviction selectively based on high index.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Mismatch branch unconditionally emits and returns.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Silent behavior replaced with observable event; partial coverage replaced with all-or-nothing.

reasoning_chain: The exploit premise relies on a silent `min()` truncation pattern (`checkLen = activeLen < perfLen ? activeLen : perfLen`) that lets validators at indices [perfLen, activeLen) bypass eviction. That pattern is NOT present in the current source. At ValidatorManagement.sol:679-682 the guard is strict equality: `if (activeLen != perfLen) { emit PerformanceLengthMismatch(activeLen, perfLen); return; }`. This guard (Step 3) is the break point. When lengths disagree, Phase 2 performance-eviction is aborted atomically for the whole set and an on-chain event signals the discrepancy — eliminating both the 'silent' and the 'trailing-validators only' aspects of the alleged attack. Underbonded eviction (Phase 1) is unaffected and still iterates over all activeLen indices without touching perfs. The spec (spec_v2/validator_management.spec.md:580) documents this behavior as intentional.

key_evidence.why_matters: Directly refutes the issue: no min() fallback, explicit event, early return — exactly the mitigation the reporter asked for.
The event the reporter claims is missing is declared and emitted.
Loop bound is activeLen (not min(activeLen, perfLen)); indexing perfs[i] is safe only because the prior equality gate enforces perfs.length == activeLen.
Shows the reset ordering; eviction is observed at Reconfiguration.sol:99/151 before this reset, so perfLen reflects the completed epoch's seeding.

counterargument: A skeptic could argue that even with the strict-equality guard, a governance-induced mid-epoch change to _activeValidators (without a corresponding PerformanceTracker seed adjustment) would still silently disable ALL performance eviction for that epoch (Phase 2 returns early). That is arguably worse than partial coverage because a single underperforming validator's mismatch now shields the entire set — potentially weaponizable if an attacker can force activeLen to drift by 1. However, (a) the code emits PerformanceLengthMismatch so operators are alerted; (b) Phase 1 underbonded eviction still runs unaffected, covering the bond-based liveness threat; (c) the only documented mid-epoch mutator paths (joinValidatorSet / leaveValidatorSet) route through PENDING_ACTIVE / PENDING_INACTIVE without changing _activeValidators length until onNewEpoch() — so drift requires an orthogonal bug not identified in this report. The original finding's exact mechanism (silent min() truncation of trailing indices) is decisively absent.
